title: volatile should be avoided to qualify objects for which the meaning is not defined
description: "volatile" should not be used to qualify objects for which the meaning is not definedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More Infovolatilecan be used to qualify many objects in C and C++, but only a few of the possible places have a well-defined meaning (global variables and local variables, for instance). There is no well-defined meaning to the use ofvolatileto qualify a function return type or a function parameter. Furthermore, for structured bindings, thevolatilequalifier appertains to the decomposed object, which cannot be referred to.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Volatile should be avoided to qualify objects for which the meaning is not defined. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
